finding.title: Multi-AI disagreement requires validation
finding.impact: No incident baseline agreement was found. 5 validation targets remain for human review, and no primary incident candidate was promoted.

agent_trace.title: Sanitize local evidence
agent_trace.summary: 6506 operational events were converted to sanitized evidence. Raw logs were not uploaded.

agent_trace.title: Verify upload boundary
agent_trace.summary: The sanitized bundle passed local verification before any model input was created.

agent_trace.title: Freeze Evidence Bundle
agent_trace.summary: The review input was fixed by SHA256 so model outputs and UI state point to the same evidence.

agent_trace.title: Run independent providers
agent_trace.summary: Gemini, gpt-oss, and Mistral produced valid outputs. Claude failed and was kept as a provider frontier signal.

agent_trace.title: Validate structure and citations
agent_trace.summary: Model claims were schema-checked and kept tied to evidence references instead of free-form root cause text.

agent_trace.title: Arbitrate disagreement
agent_trace.summary: Disagreement was preserved as validation work. No primary incident was promoted without baseline agreement.

agent_trace.title: Plan next evidence
agent_trace.summary: The agent proposed concrete validation targets for runtime recovery, traffic, configuration, observability, and liveness.

agent_trace.title: Deliver read-only review
agent_trace.summary: The Cloud URL serves a precomputed review summary first, then links to deeper persisted evidence.

devops_loop.title: AI workflow is operated as production software
devops_loop.summary: This demo exposes the run loop, failure frontier, regression profiles, and re-score boundary instead of presenting a one-shot answer.

items.label: Pipeline events
items.detail: Agent steps are persisted as observable events from sanitize through delivery.

items.label: Regression profiles
items.detail: Two operational domains are used to keep review behavior from overfitting one log shape.

items.label: Provider frontier
items.detail: Claude failure remains visible as a frontier signal instead of being hidden from reviewers.

items.label: Re-score boundary
items.detail: Additional evidence is attached to review targets, then promotion is re-evaluated behind gates.

items.label: Post-deploy smoke
items.detail: The release gate fetches both public review pages, checks required panels, and fails if the response exceeds the 10 second UI budget.

review_graph_summary.summary: 1 converged technical support signal + 5 single-source validation targets. Incident baseline remains open, so nothing was promoted to primary.
review_graph_summary.note: The converged support signal establishes shared technical context only; user impact and causal direction remain human-gated.
review_graph_summary.score_definition: Convergence score = claimed successful providers / all successful providers. Silent providers count against convergence, so one claimed and two silent providers is 1/3 = 0.333.

targets.title: Two providers converge on zero-notification context; incident impact remains open
targets.claim: OpenAI and Gemini both surfaced zero-notification or no-new-message evidence. This establishes a technical context signal, not a confirmed user-impact incident.

provider_positions.one_line: Flagged processed=0, matched=0, notified=0 run summaries with auth_status=READY.
Flagged Pub/Sub idle and no-new-message cycles as liveness context.
Did not surface this normalized support signal in the validation set.

agreement.score_definition: claimed successful providers / all successful providers
agreement.summary: Two successful providers converge on zero-notification technical context, but neither establishes expected user-visible deliveries or confirmed missed notifications.

promotion.score_note: Convergence promotes review confidence in the technical context, not incident truth.

targets.title: Watchdog activity observed; incident impact unverified
targets.claim: Systemd shows repeated watchdog start, finish, and deactivate patterns, but no provider corroborated a user-facing delivery failure.

provider_positions.one_line: Flagged watchdog and runtime recovery activity from systemd journal patterns.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.

agreement.score_definition: claimed successful providers / all successful providers
agreement.summary: Only one successful provider projected this review unit, so the graph kept it as validation work.

promotion.score_note: Priority is review urgency, not truth probability.

targets.title: Notification checks found no forwarded Amazon messages
targets.claim: Run summaries repeatedly report processed=0, matched=0, notified=0 with auth_status=READY; this is traffic context, not proof of delivery failure.

provider_positions.one_line: Flagged zero-notification traffic and requested instrumentation consistency.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.

agreement.score_definition: claimed successful providers / all successful providers
agreement.summary: Zero-notification evidence is visible, but provider overlap did not establish incident impact.

promotion.score_note: Priority is review urgency, not truth probability.

targets.title: Running service command is visible; scheduler history still missing
targets.claim: Systemd status shows the amazon-notify service loaded and running with the expected command, but scheduler history is still needed before configuration drift is accepted or rejected.

provider_positions.one_line: Flagged service configuration evidence and asked for scheduler history.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.

agreement.score_definition: claimed successful providers / all successful providers
agreement.summary: Configuration evidence was not corroborated by another successful provider.

promotion.score_note: Priority is review urgency, not truth probability.

targets.title: http_5xx labels need observability validation
targets.claim: Some patterns are labeled http_5xx while their examples are INFO run-complete or trigger-done messages; validate instrumentation before treating them as external dependency impact.

provider_positions.one_line: Flagged observability contract risk around http_5xx-labelled evidence.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.

agreement.score_definition: claimed successful providers / all successful providers
agreement.summary: The graph treats the label mismatch as an instrumentation question, not confirmed impact.

promotion.score_note: Priority is review urgency, not truth probability.

missing_evidence: Raw log files or detailed error traces for the events labeled as http_5xx.

targets.title: Pub/Sub liveness appears healthy but still single-source
targets.claim: Heartbeat-adjacent patterns show RUN_ONCE_COMPLETE, PUBSUB_IDLE_TRIGGER_DONE ok=True, and no-new-message cycles; liveness should remain validation until corroborated by another provider.

provider_positions.one_line: Flagged Pub/Sub liveness evidence as present but not enough for incident truth.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.
Did not surface this normalized target in the validation set.

agreement.score_definition: claimed successful providers / all successful providers
agreement.summary: The visible liveness target came from one provider, so it stays below primary promotion.

promotion.score_note: Priority is review urgency, not truth probability.
